text.title: Competitor Tracker — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that keep a solo competitor tracker producing signal instead of devolving into doom-scrolling competitor blogs.

rule.statement: The matrix MUST track 5-7 competitors. Fewer than 5 misses near competitors; more than 7 is too many for solo to keep current. New entrants are added only by retiring an existing entry (with rationale).

rationale.source: Crayon CI benchmarks, solo-founder time-budget research

rule.rationale: Empirically, solo founders who track > 8 competitors stop maintaining the matrix within 3 months. The 5-7 range produces sustainable maintenance with adequate coverage. The retire-to-add constraint forces deliberate prioritization.

rule.statement: Matrix columns (attributes) are defined ONCE and held stable across weeks. Forbidden: adding new attributes mid-scan to investigate a single move (creates partially-filled columns and matrix decay). New attribute additions happen only at the monthly refresh.
rule.rationale: Schema-drift kills longitudinal value. The whole point of the matrix is to see CHANGE over time per attribute. Mid-month schema changes destroy that.

rule.statement: Weekly scan MUST complete in 45 minutes wall-clock. The block is a hard timer. If 45 min runs out: stop, log "scan incomplete" with which channels weren't checked, schedule a follow-up of max 15 min if a delta needs action. The time-box is the budget; protect it.

rationale.source: Cal Newport deep-work / time-box research, solo-founder ops literature

rule.rationale: Without a time-box, competitor research expands to fill available time (Parkinson's law) and crowds out higher-leverage product / sales work. The 45-min cap forces the scan to be efficient and focused.

rule.statement: Every delta logged MUST have either (a) a queued action with date OR (b) an explicit no-op note with reason ("interesting but doesn't affect our roadmap because X"). Forbidden: deltas logged with neither — that creates a "watch list" of nothing.

rationale.source: GTD / OKR action discipline, post-research action-rate studies

rule.rationale: Delta-without-decision is noise that masquerades as signal. Forcing action-or-explicit-no-op makes the tracker either drive decisions or visibly stop being worth maintaining.

rule.statement: Once a month, prune the matrix: remove competitors whose moves haven't influenced any decision in 90 days (with reason), add at most one new competitor IF a strong candidate has emerged, review schema attributes for relevance (drop attributes that have shown no variance across competitors AND time).
rule.rationale: Without pruning, matrices accumulate stale competitors and dead attributes. Monthly prune keeps the matrix lean and the scan time fitting in 45 min.

example.title: Weekly delta journal entry with action queued
example.code:
Week of 2026-05-19 Channels scanned: changelog (5/5), pricing (5/5), blog (4/5 — missed Competitor C, queued for follow-up), social (skipped — low signal), hiring (3/5) Time: 42 min
Deltas: Competitor A: shipped "AI write-assist" feature in beta (changelog 2026-05-17). Action queued: review feature against our roadmap, decide if it changes our Q3 messaging. Owner: me. Date: 2026-05-23.
Competitor B: dropped Starter tier from $19 to $9. Action queued: re-check our pricing positioning thread; if still differentiated on Y, no change needed. Owner: me. Date: 2026-05-22.
Competitor C: blog channel not scanned this week. Action queued: 10-min catch-up scan Wednesday.
Competitor D: hiring "Founding GTM Lead". No-op with reason: hiring signal doesn't change our weekly decisions; revisit next month if pattern continues.
Competitor E: no detected change.
Matrix update: A's feature change updated in column "last-shipped". No schema changes this week.
example.note: Illustrates r2, r3, r4: stable schema, time-box respected, every delta has action OR explicit no-op.

example.title: Doom-scroll without action
example.code:
Week of 2026-05-19 Time: 2h 15min (no time-box enforced)
Notes: "Competitor A added AI thing. Competitor B did a pricing test. Competitor C wrote an article about onboarding. Competitor F (new!) launched a Product Hunt campaign. Competitor G is doing interesting things on Twitter. We should keep an eye on all of them. Maybe revisit later."
Actions queued: 0 Decisions made: 0 Time cost: 2h 15min Roadmap impact: zero
example.note: Illustrates r3, r4 violations — time bleed without decision output.
